## [wf-NA 2026-06-14] Finite-field RESTRICTION / EXTENSION (MockenhauptвАУTao / IosevichвАУKoh) вАФ PINNED to the deep-moment wall (precise, exact)

**Lens.** `M(n) = max_{bвЙ†0}|ќ£_{xвИИќЉ_n} e_p(bx)|` is the `L^вИЮ` norm of the EXTENSION operator
`(E f)(b)=ќ£_{xвИИќЉ_n} f(x) e_p(bx)` at `f=1` вАФ the finite-field restriction problem for the
multiplicative-subgroup "variety" `ќЉ_n вКВ F_q`. Tested whether the SteinвАУTomas/BourgainвАУGuth
restriction machine packages a HIGHER-`L^r` (`r>4`) or DUAL extension bound that the section-6
`L4`/energy no-go (which caps at `r=4`) misses.

**OBSTRUCTION 1 вАФ even-`r` extension norm IS the deep moment, EXACTLY (no new value).**
`вАЦE1вАЦ_{L^{2s}(F_q,counting)}^{2s} = ќ£_b |ќЈ_b|^{2s} = q¬ЈE_s` term by term. Measured exactly
(`probe_wf2NA_restriction_extension.py`, n=8,16,32, ќ≤=2..4, multi-prime, full enumeration):
`best restriction L^r M-bound вИТ best even-moment M-bound = 0.0` to machine precision at EVERY
prize prime. The `L^вИЮ`-from-`L^r` step is pure monotonicity (`max вЙ§ L^r`); H√ґlder interpolation
of the `L^r` family is log-convex вЗТ no family beats the per-`r` minimum. Restriction adds nothing
on the even-`r` (primal) side. (Brick `Frontier/_wf2NA_restriction_moment_collapse.lean`,
axiom-clean `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, no `sorryAx`: `extNorm_pow_eq_moment`,
`maxFreq_le_extNorm`.)

**OBSTRUCTION 2 вАФ the DUAL SteinвАУTomas/Mockenhaupt side is CIRCULAR (the real machine begs the
question).** The non-trivial restriction estimate needs the surface measure `dѕГ` of `ќЉ_n` to have
Fourier decay `|ќЈ_b| вЙ§ A¬Јn^{1-a'}` with `a'вЖТ1/2` (square-root cancellation = Salem). But a
PROVABLE `a'>0` IS exactly the square-root-cancellation prize `M(n)вЙ§AвИЪn` вАФ the very quantity to be
bounded. The ONLY input borrowable without begging the question is the additive energy
`EвВВ = 3n¬≤вИТ3n` (exact char-0, re-confirmed), which feeds the SteinвАУTomas estimate at its lowest
exponent `r=4` and gives EXACTLY the `L4` no-go `M(n)вЙ§(q EвВВ)^{1/4}` вАФ section-6 forbidden, one
tensor order below the loss. Measured (`probe_wf2NA_steintomas_dual.py`): the ST-`L4` bound is
WORSE than the moment ceiling (n=16,p=65537: 82.9 vs 18.8).

**OBSTRUCTION 3 вАФ `ќЉ_n` is NOT a Salem set in `F_q` (decay is arithmetic, not geometric).** The
empirical Fourier-decay exponent `a_emp = 1 вИТ log M(n)/log n` is FAR from 0.5 (measured 0.03вАУ0.21)
AND p-DEPENDENT (spread 0.03вАУ0.12 across primes at fixed n; e.g. n=16: 0.17вЖТ0.13вЖТ0.05 as ќ≤=2.5вЖТ4).
A `p`-dependent decay is an arithmetic (cyclotomic-norm-defect / BGK) property, NOT the geometric
Fourier decay restriction theory consumes. The restriction framework has no provable geometric
input for `ќЉ_n`.

**VERDICT: dead-precise-why.** Even-`r` extension = `q¬ЈE_s` verbatim (collapse, exact 0 gap);
the dual restriction theorem's only non-trivial input is `M(n)` itself (circular), and its sole
question-begging-free input (additive energy) reduces to the L4 no-go. `ќЉ_n` lacks Salem/Fourier
decay (`a_emp` p-dependent, вЙИ0.6вАУ0.97 in `kappa`). `result_type = even-r-collapses-to-deep-moment +
dual-is-circular(input=conclusion) + only-borrowable-input=L4-nogo + variety-not-Salem(decay-arithmetic-not-geometric)`.
Finite-field restriction for `ќЉ_n` вЯє the deep-moment wall. No sub-trivial `M(n)` for any prime class.

### O178 вАФ wf-ND: the theta / approximate-functional-equation route is PINNED (no contraction)

**Approach (new lens):** generalize DemirciвАУAkarsuвАУMarklof (arXiv:1207.1607) past the *quadratic*
incomplete Gauss sum to the subgroup Gauss sum `ќЈ_b = ќ£_{xвИИќЉ_n} e_p(bx)`; hope for a self-similar
theta value-distribution law whose continued-fraction-type renormalization gives a *contractive*
recursion to the floor (distinct from the false magnitude descent M¬≤вЙ§2M(n/2)¬≤ and the geometric-phase
FFT butterfly).

**Refuted premise (exact, `probe_wf2ND_theta_fe.py`, n=8..64, multi-prime incl. p/n¬≥=256, ќ≤вЙИ5):**
`{ќЈ_b/вИЪn}` is **i.i.d. complex Gaussian**, NOT the DAM theta law. Kurtosis `E|z|вБі/(E|z|¬≤)¬≤ вЖТ 2.0`
(1.94вЖТ1.98), `E|z|вБґ/(E|z|¬≤)¬≥ вЖТ 6.0`, Re-kurt вЖТ 3.0. Tail is **Rayleigh `e^{-R¬≤}`**, ~2вАУ3 orders
BELOW the DAM heavy tail `R^{-4}` at R=3 (emp 2e-5 vs R^-4=1.2e-2), vanishing past R=3.5.
`max|ќЈ|/вИЪn вЙИ 3.4` tracks Gaussian `вИЪ(2logp)`, not the DAM cusp `m^{1/4}вЙИ8вАУ16`. No self-similar law.

**Constraint lemma (proven, axiom-clean `[propext,Classical.choice,Quot.sound]`):**
`Frontier/_wf2ND_theta_fixedpoint.lean` вАФ a Fourier-inversion-type involution `T` (`T(T f)=cвАҐ(fвИШѕГ)`,
`ѕГ` bijection, `cвЙ†0`) is length-preserving: `#supp(T(T f)) = #supp f`, hence `theta_no_contraction`
`¬ђвИГf, #supp(T(T f))<#supp f`. With `T=DFT, ѕГ=neg, c=|F_p|` this is "the Poisson/theta transform does
NOT shorten the subgroup sum" вАФ verified numerically `dual_support_size = n` exactly (TEST C). The
transform is a fixed point, not a contraction; no recursion to a smaller modulus.

**result_type = value-dist-is-Gaussian-not-DAM-theta (no self-similar law) + Poisson-is-fixed-point
(no contraction, proven) + geometric-phaseвЙ†quadratic-phase (no metaplectic horocycle).** Does NOT
bound B; explains why the theta avenue cannot. Intrinsic pin, not a BGK/energy re-label.

### O179 [wf-NG] Partition-rank / multiplicative-CLP / analytic-rank PINNED on the relation count N0

**Lane:** NG (polynomial method beyond diagonal slice-rank). **Verdict: walled (NEW reason,
distinct from O21 / SliceRankDiagonalVacuous).** Attacked the ¬І407 open object
N0(mu_n,r)=#{(x_1..x_r) in mu_n^r : sum=0} via the genuinely distinct stronger tensor methods:
Croot-Lev-Pach with MULTIPLICATIVE (cyclic Z/n) indexing, Naslund partition rank, Lovett analytic
rank of the cyclotomic relation tensor S(a)=1 iff zeta^{a_1}+...+zeta^{a_r}=0.

Three machine-checked obstructions (probes probe_wf2NG_{relation_tensor_rank,clp_count_lemma}.py,
exact, char-0=char-p in clean regime p~n^5; Lean brick Frontier/_wf2NG_partition_rank_vacuous.lean
axiom-clean [propext,Classical.choice,Quot.sound]):

1. Support 100% OFF-diagonal even after multiplicative re-indexing (diag_support=0 at every n,r);
   the Tao/Naslund COUNT theorem (|X|<=r*rank for a DIAGONAL-supported tensor) bounds the empty
   diagonal -> vacuous. Multiplicative indexing does not move support off zero.
2. THE NEW reason: single-mode line-size <=1 (the favorable matching hypothesis, which the relation
   tensor SATISFIES: maxLineSize=1) yet slice/partition rank = FULL n. The method's only output
   r*n is LINEAR in n and is a FALSE upper bound on N0 (N0(mu_8,4)=168 > r*n=32; N0(mu_4,4)=36 >
   16). Proven in Lean: SingleModeMatching A does NOT imply r*n < |A| is false -- witness
   A=sumZeroSupport 4 4 has |A|=4^3=64 > r*n=16 and IS single-mode matching
   (matching_does_not_bound_count). The favorable matching does not bound the off-diagonal fiber.
3. Index group Z/2^mu is CYCLIC (d=1), not a cube (Z/2)^mu; CLP/EG exponent saving needs d->inf
   cube factors. Index-side analogue of the additive-side no-cube/Sidon-like no-go (O21).

result_type = rank-method-bounds-diagonal-not-fiber-count (the matching hypothesis is met but its
conclusion fails as an N0 bound). Genuinely new INPUT not provided; precise structural pin, not a
BGK re-label. NEXT LEVER for any future rank attempt: must bound an OFF-diagonal (r-1)-dim variety
count, which no diagonal/partition rank delivers -- would need a fundamentally non-rank polynomial
identity (e.g. a Combinatorial Nullstellensatz coefficient extraction on the relation, untried).

### P7 [2026-06-14] Explicit mu_n higher-order-MDS / BGM relaxation вАФ corank is Theta(a), NOT O(1)

**Lane:** P7-bgm-rmds-explicit (relaxed HOMDS / Brakensiek-Gopi-Makam / Roth route for the
explicit prize subgroup mu_{2^mu} at constant rate).  **Verdict: route CONFIRMED CLOSED for
explicit mu_n; the rMDS_d-with-d=O(1) crack hypothesis is REFUTED.**

The lever was: strict HOMDS needs exponential field (Brakensiek-Dhar-Gopi), but the RELAXED
rMDS_d(ell) only needs corank <= d for the SPECIFIC mu_n window configs.  If d were O(1) and
char-faithful at thick p, the list would stay generic-MDS (= O(1/rho), below capacity) and the
prize would crack.  Three machine-checked exact measurements (probes
probe_407_P7_homds_{orderL_corank,growth_law,admissibility}.py; thick primes p = 1 mod n,
log_n p in [2.5,3.5], p >> n^2, the char-0-faithful prize direction; NEVER the n=p-1 full-group
trap):

1. **char-FAITHFUL (closed form, 2400/2400 exact):** the order-ell generalized-Vandermonde
   intersection corank on a sub-subgroup-coset agreement set A = mu_a (a = 2^j | n) satisfies
   `corank_Fp(mu_a; E) = a - #distinct(e mod a)` at thick p, matching the char-0
   Schur/AbacusNCore hook-content prediction EXACTLY.  The rank-drop locus is NOT a char-p
   artifact (answers P7(b)).  Generic points have corank 0 on the same windows (drop is purely
   the multiplicative x^n=1 structure).

2. **growth law (answers P7(a)):** the genuine mu_n-specific structural excess corank GROWS
   `~ Theta(a)` at the window-interior agreement a = n/2.  Ratio L_max/(a-1) -> 1/2:
   0.667, 0.571, 0.533, 0.516, 0.508, 0.504 for n = 8,16,32,64,128,256.  d is LINEAR in the
   agreement size, NOT O(1) and NOT O(log n).

3. **list/capacity + quasipoly (answers P7(c)):** restriction rank V(mu_a; deg<k) = min(a,k)
   (236/236), so a single agreement gives a unique codeword and the HOMDS list excess is
   intrinsically the order-ell multi-set corank.  Since d = Theta(a) = Theta(n), the order
   needed to be MDS(ell) (no corank) is ell ~ corank ~ Theta(n), giving required field
   `n^{Theta(n)} = 2^{Theta(n log n)} >> prize`.  HOMDS does NOT relax to the prize field at
   the window order.

result_type = explicit-mu_n-HOMDS-corank-is-linear-in-agreement (char-faithful structural drop
of full Theta(a) scale, no O(1) relaxation).  The algebraic higher-order-MDS gap of mu_{2^mu}
EQUALS the analytic BGK wall, CONFIRMED on the explicit subgroup (not just asserted).  No crack.
NEXT LEVER for any future HOMDS attempt: would need agreement sets that are NOT sub-subgroup
cosets yet still beyond Johnson -- but the corank closed form shows the drop is exactly the
mu_a-residue collision, so any structured (orbit/coset) window inherits Theta(a) corank.

## 2026-06-14 (#407 P4): MANN/LAM-LEUNG does NOT pin the char-0 agreement-set count вАФ REFUTED
**Route under test:** the far monomial pencil `x^a + alpha*x^b` gives `f(x)=x^a+alpha*x^b-g(x)`
(`g` deg `<k`), support `{0..k-1} U {a,b}` (`<=k+2` terms). Its agreement set `R` (size `w`) =
roots of `f` in `mu_n`; each root is a vanishing sum of `<=k+2` roots of unity. Proposed closure:
for `mu_{2^mu}` (Lam-Leung) minimal vanishing relations are antipodal pairs, so the achievable
agreement sets вАФ hence the orbit/incidence count вАФ are governed by the PROVEN Mann/Lam-Leung
antipodal combinatorics (=> closeable without the BGK analytic wall).

**Test (probe `scripts/probes/probe_407_mann_agreement_count.py`, q-stable across q=262193,
2621569; char-0 faithful q>>n^4):** compare `Mann_anti(a,b;w)` = #{distinct alpha admitting an
antipodally-closed witness R} against the exact char-0 incidence `I_0(a,b;w)`.

**Result вАФ clean MISMATCH (Mann strictly undercounts in the window interior):**
  n=16 k=4 (rho=1/4):  w=5 (delta .688) I0=3696 Mann_anti=0;  w=6 (.625) I0=88 Mann_anti=24;
  w=7 (.562) I0=8 Mann_anti=0;  w=8 (.500, Johnson edge) I0=8 Mann_anti=8 EXACT;  w=9 (.438)
  I0=16 Mann_anti=0.  Mann is EXACT only at the large-`w` Johnson edge (w=n/2), where EVERY
  agreement set R is forced antipodally closed (diagnostic part (3): all w=8 R are antipodal).

**Mechanism (root cause):** `g` carries `k` FREE field coefficients and `alpha` is free, so the
vanishing sum at `zeta^j` has ARBITRARY coefficients. Lam-Leung/Mann governs vanishing sums with
+-1 / root-of-unity coefficients ONLY. With free coefficients ANY size-`(k+1)` set `R` can be
interpolated into an agreement set (at w=k+1=5, n=16: 2256 of C(16,5)=4368 subsets each force a
distinct alpha). Window-interior agreement sets are contiguous arcs like R=(0,1,2,3,4), NOT
antipodal-pair unions вАФ they are LINEAR-ALGEBRA (interpolation) realizable, not antipodal.

**Verdict:** Mann/Lam-Leung pins the agreement count ONLY in the already-known near/below-Johnson
regime. In the prize WINDOW INTERIOR (beyond Johnson, below capacity) the count is the
free-coefficient interpolation incidence I(delta) of the governing law вАФ the SAME open
BGK/counting object, NOT closeable via Mann. (Consistent with `probe_actionorbit_count.py`:
"#orbits=O(1)" is a restatement of "#bad=O(n)", the floor itself.) Probes committed:
`probe_407_mann_agreement_count.py`, `probe_407_mann_diag.py`.

## 2026-06-14 (#407 P2): RUTHLESS refutation campaign вАФ NO clean closed-form delta* survives
**Engine.** `scripts/probes/probe_407_deltastar_refute_campaign.py` computes the EXACT in-tree
governing-law delta* via the far-line incidence object `I(a,b;r) = #{gamma : x^a+gamma x^b on mu_n
agrees with some RS[k] codeword on >= n-r pts}` (epsMCA_ge_far_incidence / badScalars_eq_explainable),
no codeword enumeration / no sqrt-loss (affine-in-gamma per agreement set via left null space).
budget = q*eps* = n. Prize char-0 direction: proper mu_n (n=2^mu), p PRIME, p >> n^3; verified
char-faithful (identical maxI & binders) at p=200009/700001 (n=8) and p=200017/700001 (n=16).

**EXACT ground truth (budget=n):**
- n=16 k=1 rho=1/16: delta*=0.6875 (Johnson 0.7500) вАФ BELOW Johnson
- n=16 k=2 rho=1/8 : delta*=0.6250 (Johnson 0.6464) вАФ BELOW Johnson
- n= 8 k=1 rho=1/8 : delta*=0.6250 (Johnson 0.6464) вАФ BELOW Johnson
- n=16 k=4 rho=1/4 : delta*=0.5625 (Johnson 0.5000) вАФ ABOVE Johnson (the one +1 rung)
- n= 8 k=2 rho=1/4 : delta*=0.3750 (Johnson 0.5000) вАФ BELOW Johnson
- n= 8 k=4 / n=16 k=8 rho=1/2: far-window [k+1,n-k-1] EMPTY at budget=n => NO beyond-Johnson r.

**REFUTED:**
- **Kambire edge** `1-rho-2rho ln(1/2rho)/log2(q eps*)`: OVERSHOOTS exact delta* at 5/5 points by
  0.10вАУ0.26, and the margin does NOT shrink with n (0.13@n=8 vs 0.16@n=16 for rho=1/8). STRUCTURAL
  kill: at rho=1/2 it predicts 0.5 > Johnson 0.2929, but the exact far-window is EMPTY there
  (delta* <= Johnson). Predicts substantially MORE proximity than the law permits.
- **Entropy pin** `1-rho-H(rho)/(beta log n)` (beta=1): overshoots at n=16 k=1,2; and it is
  monotone-decreasing in rho while the exact (delta*-Johnson) FLIPS sign вАФ no single beta fixes it
  (only a fitted beta(n,rho), i.e. not a clean closed form).
- **Any "always beyond Johnson" / monotone clean form**: sign(delta*-Johnson) = [-,-,-,+,-] over
  the sweep вАФ it FLIPS. delta* is below Johnson at rho in {1/16,1/8} and at rho=1/4@n=8; above only
  at rho=1/4@n=16.
- **List ~ 2(n-a)+1**: the binding incidence is a STEP (O(1)/O(n) just below the crossover, e.g.
  maxI 5; then >>budget just above, e.g. 17вАУ18), not ~2r+1 (~23вАУ29) near the crossover.

**SURVIVOR (the only clean relation):** `|delta* - (1-sqrt(rho))| <= 1/n` holds at ALL sampled
points (n=8 k=2 exactly at the 1/n boundary). I.e. delta* = Johnson ¬± one rung вАФ there is NO simple
beyond-Johnson closed form at these computable n; the gain over Johnson (if any) is sub-rung. This is
CONSISTENT WITH THE PRIZE BEING OPEN: no candidate clean formula withstands adequate refutation.
**Honest scope:** small computable n only (full enumeration is O(C(n,n-r)) per pencil; n=32 rho=1/4
infeasible, C(32,9)=28M). Establishes NO delta* bound at prize scale (n=2^mu, mu>=10). Refutation, not
proof. Probe committed: `scripts/probes/probe_407_deltastar_refute_campaign.py` (self-checks via --compute).

## 2026-06-14 (#407 P1): char-0 I_0(delta) has NO clean rho-uniform CLOSED FORM in the window вАФ confirms BGK-open
**Question tested:** does the CHAR-0 (q-free; p>>n^3) worst-case far-line incidence
`I_0(delta) = max over far pencils (a,b>=k, b!=n/2) of #{gamma in F_p : (x^a+gamma x^b)|_{mu_n}
within Hamming dist floor(delta n) of RS[mu_n,k]}` have a CLOSED FORM (=> reduces delta* to
proven Mann/Lam-Leung), or grow uncontrollably (=> open)?

**Method (probes `probe_p1_char0.py`, `probe_p1_analysis.py`; prime-size-independent):**
exact (k+1)-subset gamma-solve + multiplicity prefilter `mult>=C(n-r,k+1)` + exact agreement test.
VALIDATED four independent ways: (k+1)-method == brute agreement-set enumerator (n=8 exhaustive,
b!=n/2); numpy histogram == pure-Python histogram (n=16, all dirs); numpy AgreeEngine ==
pure-Python agreement (n=16, 200 survivors); full pipeline reproduces probe_farline_incidence_exact
(n=16 k=4: r=10->I=89 first-bad, binder (a=10,b=4=x^k), delta*=9/16). All counts p-INDEPENDENT
(identical across 2-3 primes per point), confirming the char-0-faithful regime.

**Exact, p-independent data (budget = n):**
  n=16 k=2 (rho=1/8): I_0(r) r=8..13 = 2, 5, 5, 17, 97, 464.  delta* = 10/16 = 0.625 (first bad r=11).
  n=16 k=4 (rho=1/4): I_0(r) r=8..11 = 9, 9, 89, 3696.        delta* =  9/16 = 0.5625 (first bad r=10).
  (matches independent P4 Mann probe I0 up to the +1 gamma=0 edge: their w=6->88 vs r=10->89, etc.)

**Verdict вАФ NO clean closed form (REFUTES the Mann/Lam-Leung reduction):**
1. SUPER-POLYNOMIAL growth past the crossing: k=4 gives 9 -> 89 -> 3696 (ratios ~10x, ~42x);
   I_0/C(n,r) -> 1 only at the EXTREME high-radius boundary (r=13: 0.83), NOT in the window interior.
   No binomial/antipodal closed form (C(t,k), C(r,k), antipodal counts) matches the window values.
2. The clean candidate `delta* = (1-rho) - log2(n)/n` (committed
   `probe_char0_deltastar_pin_constrate.py`, holds for rho=1/8 at n=16,32) is RATE-SPECIFIC: the
   crossing offset n*(cap-delta*) = w*-k = 4 at rho=1/8 but 3 at rho=1/4 (both n=16, EXACT). The
   "log2(n)" is a rho=1/8 coincidence; the additive constant is rate-dependent.
3. At n=16 delta* numerically coincides with DIFFERENT closed forms per rate (k=2: 1-rho-1/log2 n;
   k=4: Johnson+1/n) -- a discretization artifact (delta* is a rung r/n; many forms land within
   +-1/32 of the granular value). NOT a genuine closed form.

**Consistency:** matches the committed P4 refutation (Mann/Lam-Leung does not pin the agreement-set
count: free `g`-coefficients + free `gamma` make the witness sets free-interpolation-realizable
arcs, not antipodal unions). The char-0 window-interior incidence is the SAME open BGK/counting
object as the field-universal sup-norm wall -- it does NOT reduce to a proven closed form.
Probes committed: `scripts/probes/probe_p1_char0.py`, `scripts/probes/probe_p1_analysis.py`.

**UPDATE (n=32 confirmation, same probes):** the rho=1/8 crossing offset w*-k = log2(n) is now
confirmed at TWO sizes (n=16: offset 4; n=32: r=23 GOOD worst I=2 binder (20,4)=x^k, r=24 BAD
worst I>=97 binder (18,4)=x^k => delta*=23/32, offset 5 = log2(32)), while rho=1/4 n=16 gives
offset 3 != log2(16)=4. So `delta*=(1-rho)-log2(n)/n` (probe_char0_deltastar_pin_constrate.py)
holds ONLY at rho=1/8 -- the crossing offset is RATE-DEPENDENT, refuting it as a rho-uniform
closed form. (n=32 used a binder-region a-scan justified by the n=16 binder=x^k low-exponent
direction; the full a-sweep is compute-bound but the binder localizes the worst pencil.)

## ORBIT-COUNT lever is RULE-3-INCOMPATIBLE (thickness-invariant) вАФ 2026-06-15

**Object:** the orbit count `N_pencil(ќі) = #orbits` of the bad-ќ± set under `ќ± вЖ¶ ќ±¬ЈѕЙ^{bвИТa}`, the governing
count in the in-tree crossing law `OrbitCountCrossingLaw.lean`: `I_pencil(ќі) вЙ§ n вЯЇ N_pencil(ќі) вЙ§ gcd(bвИТa,n)`.
The file's "Honest scope" leaves OPEN whether `N_pencil` stays bounded (O(1)) at constant rate in the
small-gap window. This is the rule-3 (thinness-essentiality) audit of that object.

**Probe:** `scripts/probes/probe_407_orbit_count_thinness.py`. Exact, subset-driven (a bad-ќ± at threshold
t is an over-determined t-subset whose order-k divided-difference windows all vanish; each DD is AFFINE in
ќ±, so per t-subset вЙ§1 candidate ќ± = common root over the tвИТk windows; union over far t-subsets = exact bad
set, NO O(q) scan). PROPER subgroups ќЉ_n < F_q*, multiple primes per (n,k) spanning thick ќ≤вЙИ2.06 вЖТ thin ќ≤вЙИ4.5.

**Result (n=8, k=2, ѕБ=1/4 = prize rate), 5 primes pвИИ{73,193,521,4073,11593}, ќ≤=2.06,2.53,3.01,4.00,4.50:**
  max-over-far-pencils orbit count at the binding band:
    thr=3 (ќі=0.625): maxN = 6 вАФ IDENTICAL across all 5 ќ≤ (same extremal pencil (2,6)).
    thr=4 (ќі=0.500): maxN = 2 вАФ IDENTICAL.
    thr=5 (ќі=0.375): maxN = 1 вАФ IDENTICAL.
  EXACTLY thickness-invariant: same value, same extremal direction, ќ≤=2.06 вЖТ 4.50.

**Confirmation (n=16, k=4, ѕБ=1/4, thr=7 = the ќі*=9/16 binding band):**
  THICK p=257 (ќ≤=2.00): maxN=2, extremal pencil (4,12).
  THIN  p=65537 (ќ≤=4.00, Fermat): pencil (4,12) maxN=2, I=4 вАФ IDENTICAL on the worst direction.

**Verdict:** `max_pencils N_pencil` at the binding band is thickness-invariant вЯє a thickness-monotone
orbit-count bound CANNOT prove CORE (false in the thick window, rule 3). The orbit-count REFORMULATION
inherits the SAME rule-3 obstruction as the moment/energy family: the crossing law is an exact, axiom-clean,
BGK-FREE structural restatement, but the governing count is thickness-invariant вАФ the orbit basis buys
structure, not a thinness discriminator. Extends the route-elimination map to the orbit-count brick (#5).

**Nuance (honest):** the free-action factorization `I = N¬ЈS` holds on the extremal pencil (verified n=8
pencil (2,6): I=12=6¬Ј2, all orbits size 2, ќ±=0вИЙbad, p-independent across p=73,4073) but `factOK` over the
FULL sweep goes False at low thr вАФ some pencils have mixed orbit sizes (degenerate near-directions /
fixed-point orbit), so the clean `I=N¬ЈS` is a per-pencil (worst-direction) statement, looser than a uniform
hypothesis. Consistent with the file's ќ±=0-fixed-point caveat; the in-tree theorems remain correct.

**Scope:** small n (8 full band, 16 worst-pencil); maps the rule-3 CHARACTER of the orbit-count object,
does NOT prove the asymptotic. p-independence of the count is consistent with the campaign's PROVEN
p-independence. No Lean change (empirical thickness-invariance; proving const-slack would be BGK). No CORE
closure. Probe-first, exact, honest.

## BoundedHalfBasisIndep (the named char-p prize core): FALSE at the prize prime + thinness signal CLOSES at large n вАФ 2026-06-15

**Object:** the freshest unification's named open core (`BoundedCyclotomicIndep.lean`, `BridgeBounded.lean`,
`AntipodalBalanceBounded.lean`, capstone `CountAntipodalBounded.lean`):
> `BoundedHalfBasisIndep ѕЙ N C` := no nonzero `g вИИ [вИТC,C]^N` (N = 2^{m-1} = n/2) with `ќ£_j g_j ѕЙ^j = 0` mod p.
> Chain: `BoundedHalfBasisIndep вЯє count-antipodal вЯє char-p Wick ladder вИАr вЯє CORE`. "Prize = that one named
> hypothesis at prize support вАФ open = BGK." `BridgeBounded` claims the chain needs only **C = 4**, "independent
> of tuple size". `BoundedCyclotomicIndep` says prize support is `C ~ 2 ln q вЙИ 22`.

This is the rule-3 (thinness-essentiality) + at-the-prize-prime audit of that object. Define
`C*(n,p) = min max|g_j|` over nonzero half-basis relations (BHBI(ѕЙ,N,C) is TRUE iff `C < C*`).

**Probes (exact, PROPER subgroups ќЉ_n вКК F_p*, prize ќ≤вЙИ4, never n=qвИТ1):**
`scripts/probes/probe_407_bounded_cyclotomic_indep.py` (MITM exact small-box),
`probe_407_bhbi_verify_n16.py` (independent full-brute, dual-engine), `probe_407_bhbi_verify_n32.py`
(LLL shortest + exact integer verification), `probe_407_bhbi_rule3_thinness.py` (thin-vs-thick control).

**RESULT 1 вАФ C* erodes BELOW the chain's required C=4 at the prize prime, at fixed ќ≤=4:**
| n  | prize prime pвЙИnвБі | ќ≤    | C*(thin) | chain needs C=4 |
|----|------------------|------|----------|-----------------|
| 8  | 3457вАУ7681        | ~4.0 | **7вАУ8**  | HOLDS (margin)  |
| 16 | 65537            | 4.00 | **4**    | **BREAKS (C*=4)** |
| 32 | 1048609,1179649  | ~4.0 | **1**    | **BREAKS (C*=1)** |

**DUAL-ENGINE VERIFIED (n=16, p=65537, ѕЙ=64, primitive 16th root, ѕЙ^8=вИТ1):** independent full-brute
confirms `BHBI(ѕЙ,8,3)` TRUE (no relation, all |g|вЙ§3) and `BHBI(ѕЙ,8,4)` FALSE with explicit witness
**g=(вИТ4,вИТ4,вИТ4,вИТ1,вИТ1,вИТ1,0,0)**, exact `ќ£ g_j ѕЙ^j = вИТ196611 = вИТ3¬Ј65537 вЙ° 0`. So **C*(n=16,prize prime)=4
exactly** вЯє the chain's `BoundedHalfBasisIndep ѕЙ 8 4` is **precisely, machine-checkably FALSE**, refuting
the `BridgeBounded` "C=4 suffices, independent of tuple size" required-support claim AT the prize prime.
**n=32 (p=1048609,1179649):** verified height-1 sign-relations `ќ£ ¬±ѕЙ^j = ¬±p` (support 11/16, ѕЙ genuine
primitive 32nd root) вЯє even `BHBI(ѕЙ,16,1)` is FALSE.

**RESULT 2 (the rule-3 wall вАФ the real map) вАФ the THINNESS signal of C* CLOSES at large n:**
Control: thin 2-power ѕЙ half-basis vs generic THICK config of same size N, same prime, median/5 seeds.
| n  | p (ќ≤вЙИ4)  | thin C* | thick C* (med) | verdict |
|----|----------|---------|----------------|---------|
| 16 | 65537    | 4       | 2              | **THIN>thick (thinness-essential)** |
| 32 | 1048609  | 1       | 1              | **TIE (NOT essential)** |
| 32 | 33554593 (ќ≤=5) | 2 | 2          | **TIE (NOT essential)** |

C* is a thinness discriminator at n=16 (thin 4 vs thick 2) but the gap **closes by n=32** (thin==thick,
at both ќ≤=4 and ќ≤=5). At N=16 the prize-regime prime (p~nвБі~10вБґ) is small enough that ~3^16 sign-vectors
into ~10вБґ residues FORCE a short relation for ANY config (Minkowski/pigeonhole) вАФ the 2-power structure
buys no detectable independence advantage.

**CONSTRAINT LEMMA / VERDICT:** the `BoundedHalfBasisIndep` lever, while it CORRECTLY NAMES the open core
(`BoundedHalfBasisIndep вЯє Wick ladder вЯє CORE` is a sound reduction), is (a) FALSE at the prize prime
already at small C (C*=4 @ n=16, C*=1 @ n=32 вАФ far below both C=4 and 2 ln q), and (b) its C* becomes
THICKNESS-(near-)INVARIANT at large n (thin==thick by n=32). So **a thinness-monotone bound on C* CANNOT
prove CORE** вАФ the C*-of-the-half-basis object inherits the SAME rule-3 obstruction as the moment / energy
/ orbit-count family. The named hypothesis correctly localizes the wall but cannot be discharged by a C*
(bounded-relation-height) thinness argument: the discriminating content lives ABOVE the bounded-relation
floor, not in it. Mapped wall (rule-4 WIN), not a closure.

**HONEST SCOPE:** C* upper bounds via LLL at nвЙ•32 (exact verify of each witness; exact MITM at nвЙ§16).
Small n (8,16 exact; 32,64 LLL-UB). The thin==thick TIE at n=32 is robust across two primes; the n¬≤вБі/nвБґ
LLL row was an LLL-resolution artifact (no relation found in thick configs at the largest prime) and is
EXCLUDED. Does NOT refute the prize (the reduction is sound; the hypothesis being false-where-needed IS the
BGK wall). No Lean change (empirical refutation + thickness-invariance map; axiom-clean trivially). No CORE
closure. Probe-first, dual-engine on the headline, honest on the rule-3 closure.

### STRENGTHENING (same lever, finer invariant): the height-1 relation COUNT is also non-discriminating вАФ 2026-06-15
Follow-up to the C* closure above. A finer invariant than C\* is the NUMBER of height-1 (sign) relations
`#{gвИИ{вИТ1,0,1}^N : ќ£ g_j ѕЙ^j = 0}`. If thin had systematically FEWER, the bounded-indep deficit would be
graded (a surviving thinness signal). Probe `scripts/probes/probe_407_bhbi_relcount.py` (exact, vectorized
3^16 count). **Result (n=32, N=16, FOUR prize primes pвЙИnвБі, ќ≤=4.000):**
  | p | thin # | thick # (med/5) | direction |
  |---|--------|------------------|-----------|
  | 1048609 | 32 | 16 | THIN>thick (anti-rule-3!) |
  | 1048897 | 16 | 24 | thin<thick (rule-3) |
  | 1049057 | 32 | 19 | THIN>thick |
  | 1049089 | 16 | 21 | thin<thick |
The count SIGN-FLIPS above/below thick across primes at FIXED ќ≤=4 вАФ NO monotone thinness direction. At
the prize prime the 2-power coset's arithmetic (closure under doubling: ѕЙ^{2j} relations) can even create
MORE low-height relations than a generic set (anti-rule-3). Confirms decisively: neither C\* NOR the
bounded-relation count is a thinness discriminator in the prize regime вЯє the bounded-cyclotomic-independence
lever is rule-3-incompatible at large n by both invariants. (n=16: zero sign-relations for thin AND thick,
consistent with C\*>1 there.) Strengthens the mapped wall; no new receipt (same object as push 1fa2d5e58).

## EvenDirectionIncidenceCollapse (reverse 2-adic descent leg) is FALSE вАФ 2026-06-15

**Object.** `EvenDirectionDescent.lean` proves the FORWARD leg of the 2-adic even-direction far-line
incidence descent unconditionally: `I_{n/2}` injects into `I_n(even direction x^{2a'})`
(`explainableScalars_sq_pullback_subset`). The clean prize-useful identity `I_n(even) = I_{n/2}` needs
the REVERSE containment `I_n(even) вКЖ I_{n/2}`, named as the open Prop `EvenDirectionIncidenceCollapse`
(consumed only as a hypothesis in `explainableScalars_sq_pullback_eq_of_collapse`). The file's honest
caveat: the `RS[ќЉ_n,2k]` codeword explaining a full even-direction line need not be the even pullback of
an `RS[ќЉ_{n/2},k]` codeword вАФ its ODD part can carry agreement on a non-fibre-symmetric witness set.

**REFUTATION (exact, `scripts/probes/probe_407_even_direction_collapse.py`).** n=16, k=2, binding band
(half threshold s'=3, full threshold s=6 = 2s' per the fibre-doubling budget), far even line
`(x^{2k}, x^{2(k+1)})` on ќЉ_16 vs `RS[ќЉ_16,4]` against the descended `(x^k, x^{k+1})` on ќЉ_8 vs `RS[ќЉ_8,2]`:

| p   | \|I_n(even)\| | \|I_{n/2}\| | fwd (halfвКЖfull) | rev (fullвКЖhalf) | EQ |
|-----|---------------|-------------|------------------|------------------|----|
| 97  | 56            | 40          | TRUE             | **FALSE**        | no |
| 113 | 40            | 40          | TRUE             | TRUE             | yes|

At p=97 there are **16 scalars bad for the full even direction on ќЉ_16 but NOT bad on ќЉ_8**.
INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED (exact max-agreement, `verify`): ќ≥ вИИ {14,17,21,23} each have full-domain
max-agreement = **6** (вЙ• 6 вЯє bad against deg<4) while the squaring-descent half-line has max-agreement
only **2** (< 3 вЯє NOT bad against deg<2). So the agreement-6 on ќЉ_16 is realized by an `RS[ќЉ_16,4]`
codeword whose odd part supplies вЙ•4 of the 6 agreement points on a non-fibre-symmetric set that does not
descend вАФ exactly the named caveat, now with explicit witnesses.

**CONSTRAINT LEMMA / VERDICT.** `EvenDirectionIncidenceCollapse` is FALSE in general (prime-dependent:
fails at p=97, holds at p=113 for the same n,k,band). Therefore the clean 2-adic evenвАУodd identity
`I_n(even) = I_{n/2}` does NOT hold, and the squaring descent is genuinely **one-sided** (forward lift
only). Any descent/Stepanov argument that consumes the reverse collapse as a step is blocked: the
even-direction incidence on ќЉ_n strictly EXCEEDS `I_{n/2}` at the binding band (odd-part agreement is
non-descending). The forward leg `I_n(even) вЙ• I_{n/2}` is the wrong direction for the prize upper bound,
and the reverse leg that would fix it is now refuted. Mapped wall (rule-4 WIN), not a closure; no Lean
change (the open Prop stands correctly named вАФ this records that it is FALSE, so it cannot be discharged).
Probe-first, exact, dual-checked. Does NOT refute the prize (the forward brick is sound; the reverse
identity it leaves open is simply false, which is itself the obstruction).

## DFT-uncertainty quantitative core: classical UP bound VACUOUS + genuine far-line max-zeros is SUB-Johnson (two-small-coset), with the n/2-growth EXPOSED as the ќЉ_{n/2} even-subgroup artifact вАФ 2026-06-15

**Lane.** Adversarial audit of the freshest conceptual frame (KB `INSIGHT-smooth-domain-hardness-IS-the-
uncertainty-principle.md`): the prize restated as the quantitative DFT-uncertainty question for Z_{2^ќЉ} вАФ
for f on Z_n with DFT-support T={0..kвИТ1,a,b}, is max-zeros s* = вИЪ(kn) (Johnson) or k+ќШ(n/log n) (floor)?
The INSIGHT asserts "smooth n=2^ќЉ вЯє s* grows ~вИЪ(kn) (Johnson)". Tested EXACTLY (rank characterization,
no coeff search), proper ќЉ_n, prize-band pвЙ°1(n), index m=(pвИТ1)/nвЙ•2, multi-prime (incl. Fermat F_4=65537),
NEVER n=qвИТ1, k=3.

**Object (exact).** s*(n,p,a,b) = max|Z|, ZвКЖZ_n, with rank([w^{tz}]_{zвИИZ,tвИИT}) < |T|=k+2 (a nonzero
T-spectral f vanishing on Z). Computed exactly over every (k+1)-subset = every minimal-support codeword,
maximized over far-line directions (a,b).

**RESULT 1 вАФ classical uncertainty LOWER bound is VACUOUS for the prize.** DonohoвАУStark
|supp f|¬Ј|supp f^| вЙ• n with |supp f^|=k+2 вЯє s* вЙ§ n(1вИТ1/(k+2)) = 0.8n (k=3). That is a FULL POWER OF n
ABOVE Johnson вИЪ(kn) (n=2^20: DS-permitted 838860 vs Johnson 1773). Tao's strong UP (s*вЙ§k+1=capacity)
holds ONLY for n PRIME, not 2^ќЉ. вЯє the "weak UP" frame is NECESSARY-NOT-SUFFICIENT: weak UP only permits
large s*, gives NO вИЪ(kn) ceiling. The uncertainty frame, via known quantitative bounds, does not separate
Johnson from floor as a theorem. Probe: `scripts/probes/probe_407_uncertainty_bound.py`.

**RESULT 2 вАФ the INSIGHT's "smooth s*вЖТJohnson/n-2 growth" is DEGENERATE (two-layer rule-2 trap, exact).**
Maxing over directions gave ALL s*=6,10,18 (s*вИТk=3,7,15) at n=8,16,32 binding at a=n/2 вАФ but witness audit
EXPOSES these as subgroup-supported:
- Layer 1: a=n/2 (antipodal / I=qвИТ1 pencil) inflates s* by EXACTLY +1, p-independent across 4 primes each.
- Layer 2 (the trap prior aвЙ†n/2 filters MISS): the s*вЙИn/2 values are the EVEN-SUBGROUP ќЉ_{n/2}=вЯ®2вЯ© vanishing
  set. n=32 dir(17,18) "nondeg" (a,bвЙ†16, gcd odd) s*=17 witness = {all 16 EVEN residues}вИ™{1} = ќЉ_16 + 1 pt.
  When a or b sits near n/2, w^{ax} is ~constant on the even subgroup вЯє rank collapse вЯє spurious n/2 zeros.
  Correct degeneracy notion = WITNESS contains a вЙ•n/4 subgroup-coset (NOT just aвЙ†n/2).
Probe: `scripts/probes/probe_407_uncertainty_witness_audit.py` (exact witness dump + subgroup/antipodal test).

**RESULT 3 вАФ the GENUINE (subgroup-saturation-excluded) far-line max-zeros is SUB-Johnson, two-small-coset.**
Excluding witnesses containing a вЙ•n/4 subgroup-coset, exact, multi-prime:
| n  | genuine s* | s*вИТk | Johnson вИЪ(kn) | witness structure |
|----|-----------|------|---------------|-------------------|
| 8  | 4         | 1    | 4.90          | interval {0,1,2,3} |
| 16 | 6         | 3    | 6.93          | two ќЉ_2-cosets {0,1,2}вИ™{8,9,10} |
| 32 | 8         | 5    | 9.80          | two ќЉ_4-cosets {0,1,8,9,16,17,24,25} |
Genuine s* = 4,6,8 (s*вИТk=1,3,5, +2 per doubling) sits STRICTLY BELOW Johnson at all three n, gap WIDENING
(Johnson √ЧвИЪ2 per doubling vs genuine +2). Binding witness is ALWAYS two cosets of a SMALL subgroup. This
INDEPENDENTLY CONFIRMS c.348 (far-line max-agreement вЙ§ Johnson, cannot reach floor 1вИТѕБвИТќШ(1/log n)) from the
DFT-uncertainty side, and pins the EXACT witness mechanism (super-(k+1) far-line agreement on ќЉ_{2^ќЉ} comes
ONLY from low-subgroup coset blocks: saturated вЯє n/2 degeneracy; two-small-coset вЯє sub-Johnson).
Probes: `scripts/probes/probe_407_maxzeros_exact.py`, `probe_407_maxzeros_sweep.py`.

**VERDICT (mapped constraint, rule-4 WIN; CORE not closed, not faked).** Corrects the INSIGHT KB ("smooth s*
grows ~вИЪ(kn)" вАФ the apparent growth is the ќЉ_{n/2} subgroup artifact; the GENUINE far-line s* is SUB-Johnson)
and nails the exact TWO-LAYER degeneracy (a=n/2 вЖТ +1; even-subgroup witness вЖТ +n/2) that has been generating
false floor-signals in the uncertainty/lacunary lanes. The classical uncertainty LOWER bound (DS/Meshulam) is
vacuous (off by a full power of n). Does NOT refute the prize: it confirms (from a new, c.348-immune PROOF-
object angle) that the far-line/uncertainty face cannot reach the floor; the open core remains the per-frequency
BGK вИЪ-cancellation wall. p-independent, exact, multi-prime, proper subgroups, never n=qвИТ1.

## wf-NK (#444): Khovanskii / fewnomial bound on the binding over-determined incidence вАФ REFUTED as a tight bound

**Lens.** The binding far-line incidence `I(a,b;r*)` counts ќ≥ such that `f_ќ≥(X)=X^a+ќ≥X^bвИТh(X)`
(`hвИИRS[k]`, deg<k) vanishes on вЙ• nвИТr* points of ќЉ_n. `f_ќ≥` is a SPARSE Laurent polynomial:
support `{a,b,0,вА¶,kвИТ1}` = at most `m=k+2` distinct exponents (CONFIRMED exact on all binders).
Khovanskii fewnomial theory bounds solution counts of a sparse system by the number of monomials
`m`, degree-free and field-free вАФ formally the right regime for the now-combinatorial object.

**Measured binding data (exact, p-independent; n=16 in-tree verified e167dd9de):**
- n=8 k=2: r*=4 size=4 binder(5,2) `I=9`, support {0,1,2,5}, m=4=k+2
- n=12 k=3: r*=6 size=6 binder(6,4) `I=13`, support {0,1,2,4,6}, m=5=k+2
- n=16 k=4: r*=10 size=6 binder(10,4) `I=89` (PRIME), support {0,1,2,3,4,10}, m=6=k+2

**Why it fails (precise, two-sided):**
1. Classical Khovanskii `2^C(m,2)¬Ј(n_var+1)^m` (1 eliminated ќ≥-variable) = 1024/32768/2097152 вАФ
   OVERSHOOTS I by 100√ЧвАУ24000√Ч and grows EXPONENTIALLY in k. Gives ќі*вЖТ1: vacuous.
2. Descartes-on-circle / cyclic `2(mвИТ1)=2(k+1)` = 6/8/10 UNDERSHOOTS I=9/13/89 вАФ it caps
   roots-PER-ќ≥, not the COUNT of ќ≥, so it is not even a valid upper bound on I.
3. I(n) is NOT a fewnomial-polynomial sequence: I(16)=89 is PRIME; the (n/2вИТ1)¬≤ form (9,25,49)
   is REFUTED by it (matches n=8 only coincidentally). A degree-free fewnomial bound is a
   *polynomial* in (n,k); a non-polynomial prime-valued target cannot be matched tightly.
4. The fewnomial cap governs the per-ќ≥ root count, but I is a UNION over C(n,size) witnesses; the
   distinctness collapse (89 distinct out of 8008 size-6 witnesses, n=16) is governed by how the
   forced ќ≥'s COINCIDE вАФ the additive/multiplicative structure of ќЉ_n, i.e. the BGK residual the
   lens is structurally blind to.
5. The sharpest fewnomial-on-a-cyclic-group bound (Tao uncertainty: nonzero fn on вД§/n with t
   nonzero Fourier coeffs has вЙ§ nвИТt zeros) requires n PRIME; for n=2^ќЉ it is VACUOUS. The prize's
   defining smooth highly-composite domain is exactly what defeats the fewnomial/uncertainty lens
   (corroborates in-tree 6507e61aa).

**Net:** the loosest VALID fewnomial bound is `C(n,size)` (= the NH union bound itself); fewnomial
theory adds nothing tighter. Probe: `scripts/probes/probe_wf3NK_fewnomial_incidence.py` (self-
contained, fast). Tag: **refuted** as a tight degree-free/p-free bound on I(n); the (k+2)-sparsity
and the precise failure mode are proven-per-fixed-n. вАФ wf-NK

## cosh-MGF / exact-Bessel-saddle CORE bound CAPS at вИЪ(ќ≤/(ќ≤вИТ1))¬Јfloor вАФ "beats at n=8" is a small-n artifact (¬І7.8, #444) вАФ 2026-06-15

**Lane.** ¬І7.8 of #444: the cosh-MGF identity (`Frontier/CoshMGFIdentity.lean`, axiom-clean in-tree)
`ќ£_b cosh(вАЦќЈ_bвАЦy) = ќ£_r (q¬ЈE_r/(2r)!) y^{2r}` gives, by per-b domination, the root-free max-free CORE
upper bound `M(n) = max_bвАЦќЈ_bвАЦ вЙ§ B(y) = arccosh(ќ£_b cosh(вАЦќЈ_bвАЦy))/y` (вИАy>0). For G=ќЉ_n the char-0
even-moment GF is the Bessel law (`DyadicEnergyK1.lean`): `ќ£_r E_r y^{2r}/(2r)! = IвВА(2y)^{n/2}`, so the
CHAR-0-IDEALIZED bound (the BEST the mechanism gives IF char-0 energies held char-p) is
`B_char0(n,p) = min_{y>0} arccosh(p¬ЈIвВА(2y)^{n/2})/y`. The dossier (¬І7.8) flags it as beating the floor
`вИЪ(2n log m)` at n=8 (8.56<9.99). NO prior worker probed whether this survives to larger n.

**RESULT 1 вАФ "beats at n=8" is a SMALL-n ARTIFACT; the bound asymptotes to ~1.155√Чfloor, never AT it.**
PART A (ќ≤=4, p~n^4, m~n^3, char-0-idealized, wide-saddle):
| n | floor | B_char0 | B/floor | verdict |
|---|-------|---------|---------|---------|
| 8 | 9.99 | 7.90 | 0.791 | BEATS |
| 16 | 16.32 | 15.06 | 0.923 | BEATS |
| 32 | 25.80 | 26.58 | 1.030 | WORSE |
| 256 | 92.29 | 105.75 | 1.146 | WORSE |
| 16384 | 976.7 | 1137.1 | 1.164 | WORSE |
| 1048576 | 9339 | 10851 | 1.162 | WORSE |
B/floor вЖТ ~1.16 (a CONSTANT factor ABOVE the floor), never reaching it. BEATS only nвЙ§16 at ќ≤=4
(nвЙ§512 at fixed prize m=2^128, then crosses + grows). The n=8 BEATS is finite-n only.

**RESULT 2 вАФ MECHANISM (analytic): the saddle COLLAPSES the exact Bessel to its GAUSSIAN/Wick leading term.**
At small y, `log IвВА(2y) = y¬≤ вИТ (1/4)yвБі + вА¶`, so `(n/2)log IвВА(2y) ~ (n/2)y¬≤` вАФ THE WICK TERM (the вИТyвБі/4
curvature is lower-order). Then `B ~ min_y [log p + (n/2)y¬≤]/y = вИЪ(2n log p)` at `y* = вИЪ(2 log p/n)`.
Floor = `вИЪ(2n log m)`. So **B_char0/floor вЖТ вИЪ(log p/log m) = вИЪ(ќ≤/(ќ≤вИТ1))** (m~n^{ќ≤вИТ1}, p~n^ќ≤). ќ≤=4 вЯє
**вИЪ(4/3)=1.1547** (numeric 1.16); ќ≤=5 вЯє 1.118. NUMERIC CONFIRMATION (B_char0 vs вИЪ(2n log p), ќ≤=4):
MATCH to <0.5% at n=256..16M. The Bessel curvature (the only place a thin advantage could live) is
provably LOWER-ORDER and cannot close the log p/log m ratio.

**RESULT 3 вАФ the char-0 bound is a VALID upper bound on the TRUE char-p M (PART B, exact FFT, multi-prime).**
n=8 at p=4129/28697/2000081 and n=16,32 at prize-band primes: B_char0 вЙ• M_true (FFT period sup) at
every prime вАФ the idealized bound is a genuine over-estimate of the real periods, so the cap is on a
VALID upper-bound mechanism, not a broken bound.

**RESULT 4 вАФ RULE-3 thinness gate (PART C): thin-sensitive but only LOWER-ORDER.**
Thin 2-power Bessel `IвВА(2y)^{n/2}` vs Gaussian/Wick `e^{ny¬≤/2}` feeding the SAME bound: THIN-BEATS-GAUSS
at EVERY n (n=8: 8.31<12.01; n=32768: 1664.1<1664.9). So the IвВА curvature IS a genuine thin advantage
over generic-Wick (rule-3 PASS), but the gap thin-vs-gauss shrinks relative to the floor as n grows вЯє
insufficient: the thin win is real but cannot cross the вИЪ(ќ≤/(ќ≤вИТ1)) wall.

**VERDICT (rule-4 mapped wall; CORE not closed, not faked).** The cosh-MGF / exact-Bessel-saddle
mechanism (¬І7.8) caps at **вИЪ(ќ≤/(ќ≤вИТ1))¬Јfloor** (вЙИ1.155√Ч at ќ≤=4), NEVER at the floor, EVEN granted the
char-0 energies вАФ so it fails BEFORE the ¬І2 char-p DC-term obstruction (independent of it). The dossier's
"beats the floor at n=8" is a genuine small-n artifact. The decisive structural reason: at the saddle the
exact Bessel `(n/2)log IвВА(2y)` collapses to its Wick leading term `(n/2)y¬≤` вАФ so this IS the ¬І4
second-order/meta-theorem wall at the MGF level, now QUANTIFIED as the вИЪ(ќ≤/(ќ≤вИТ1)) log-ratio gap. Removes
¬І7.8 as a live hope: the cosh-MGF is a sharper SUBSTRATE but not a sharper BOUND. Probe:
`scripts/probes/probe_407_cosh_bessel_saddle_scaling.py` (wide geometric-grid saddle, large-z IвВА
asymptotic, exact-FFT char-p cross-check, rule-3 thinness gate, analytic confirmation). Axiom-clean
(Python-only, no Lean changed). Co-authored wakesync.

## [C09] Jacobi-Sum Diagonal Energy Closure for the 6th Moment (r=3 single-rung) вАФ REFUTED (false claim) / reduces-to-trivial

**Conjecture.** Express E_3(ќЉ_n) via Jacobi sums (|J|=вИЪp) + order-8 cyclotomic numbers, claim
E_3(ќЉ_{2^ќЉ}) вЙ§ 15n¬≥ exactly in char p above the order-8 Jacobi-degeneracy resultant, and thereby
"pin the r=3 rung of M(ќЉ_n) вЙ§ вИЪ(2n log p)."

**VERDICT: the load-bearing claim is FALSE.** There is no "r=3 rung of вИЪ(2n log p)". The moment chain
(`GaussPeriodMomentBound.worstCaseIncompleteSumBound_of_energyBound`) gives, from a single rung r,
`M_r = (q¬Ј(2r-1)вАЉ¬Јn^r)^{1/2r}`. The formula вИЪ(2n log p) is the value AFTER minimizing over r at the
optimum r* вЙИ ln q. A single fixed rung r=3 gives `M_3 = (q¬Ј15¬Јn¬≥)^{1/6}`, dominated by `q^{1/6}`.

**Quantitative kill (probe `scripts/probes/probe_c09_r3_rung_useless.py`, commit 0ba4197bb):**
prize regime n=2^30, q=n^ќ≤:
- ќ≤=4 (q=2^120): r=3 gives M_3 = 2^{5.65}¬Јn вЙИ **50¬Јn** вАФ WORSE than the trivial bound M вЙ§ n.
- ќ≤=5 (q=2^150): r=3 gives M_3 = 2^{10.65}¬Јn вЙИ **1608¬Јn** вАФ far worse than trivial.
- M_r first beats trivial (M<n) only at r=5 (ќ≤=4) / r=10 (ќ≤=5); reaches the вИЪ(2n log p)вЙИ12.9вИЪn
  target only at r* вЙИ ln q вЙИ 83 (ќ≤=4) / 104 (ќ≤=5).
- Algebraic: r=3 beats trivial iff q < n¬≥/15, i.e. ќ≤ < 3 вАФ OUTSIDE the prize band (ќ≤вЙ•4). So in the
  prize regime r=3 NEVER beats trivial, let alone reaches Johnson or capacity.

**Even granting E_3 вЙ§ 15n¬≥ in char p, r=3 pins NOTHING past Johnson** вАФ it sits on the trivial/negative
side. And the E_3 вЙ§ 15n¬≥ char-p step is itself the SAME open residual `RepThree` char-p transfer
(`GaussianEnergyThreeRepThree.lean` reduces `GaussianEnergyBound G 3` to the order-6 antipodal-pairing
residual; the Jacobi/cyclotomic-number reframing is a different bookkeeping of that same char-0вЖТchar-p
crossing, still open at prize scale). So the conjecture is doubly non-closing: (1) its arithmetic
("r=3 rung of вИЪ(2n log p)") is FALSE, and (2) its only nontrivial content (E_3 char-p) is the named
open residual, not new proven math.

Probe: `scripts/probes/probe_c09_r3_rung_useless.py` (commit 0ba4197bb). Python-only; no Lean changed.

## [C23] GM-MDS Dual-Zero-Pattern Certificate for ќЉ_n вАФ SECRETLY-OPEN (existence-vs-fixed horn)

**Date:** 2026-06-14. **Verdict:** `secretly-open` (the step that would carry it past Johnson is
the open one; for the GM-MDS certificate over fixed ќЉ_n it is provably FALSE вАФ the certificate
vanishes). **Probe:** `scripts/probes/probe_c23_gmmds_fixed_vs_generic.py`, commit `ce07c4033`.

**The claim.** Encode the L+1 candidate codewords as a GM-MDS dual zero-pattern Z; it satisfies the
BrakensiekвАУGopiвАУMakam |Z_S| вЙ§ (kвИТ1)|S| solvability criterion (because the far-line agreement set is
ќЉ_gcd-coset-closed, orbit-count law L3), so Lovett Thm 1.7 gives realizability at field size вЙ§ (kвИТ1)!
< q, capping the list at kвИТ1 and pinning ќі* = capacity вИТ ќШ(1/log n).

**The defect (existence-vs-fixed).** In-tree `LovettThm17 F n` is a *generic/symbolic* statement:
the union polynomial family `pFamUnion(V,k)` is `LinearIndependent` over `MvPolynomial (Fin n) F` вАФ
i.e. the symbolic generalized-Vandermonde determinant (a polynomial in the *eval points*) is not the
zero polynomial, so the pattern is realizable for SOME (generic) choice of points, and field size
вЙ§ (kвИТ1)! is the number of points the construction is *free to pick*. The prize FIXES the eval set to
ќЉ_n (a proper subgroup). The relevant certificate is then that symbolic det *evaluated* at the fixed
x_i вИИ ќЉ_n. C23 conflates "realizable for some eval set" with "realizable for the prescribed ќЉ_n".

**The wall (already in-tree).** At the fixed smooth domain ќЉ_n the certificate `det(ќґ^{ќ≤_j¬Јi})` is
nonzero **iff** the abacus n-core of the shape is empty (`HOMDSSmoothObstruction.homds_det_ne_zero_iff_nCoreEmpty`),
and for the past-Johnson extremal shape (a rectangle a^h, h = L < n codewords) that holds **iff n | a**
(`RectNCore.rectBeta_nCoreEmpty_iff`). Generic list widths (n вИ§ a) вЖТ NONEMPTY core вЖТ the fixed-ќЉ_n
certificate VANISHES. The cyclic symmetry x^n=1 that makes the domain "smooth" is exactly what
annihilates the GM-MDS certificate in the capacity regime.

**Probe evidence (proper ќЉ_n, p prime вЙИ 64¬Јn¬≥ вЙЂ n¬≥, n < pвИТ1, NEVER n = pвИТ1).** For n вИИ {16,32,64},
k = n/2, every list height h вИИ {2,4,k}, every past-Johnson width a (Johnson agreement and Johnson+1):
- GM-MDS criterion: granted True. Symbolic det at GENERIC random huge-prime points: nonzero (Lovett
  applies, generic realizable).
- **The SAME det evaluated at the fixed ќЉ_n over F_p: ZERO** for every n вИ§ a (the past-Johnson regime).
  It is nonzero only at the n | a "lucky" widths, which are not the adversarial list shapes.
The in-tree dichotomy `nCoreEmpty(a^h) вЗФ n|a вЗФ ќЉ_n-det вЙ† 0` reproduced over fixed ќЉ_n with 0 mismatches.

**Why not `reduces-to-johnson`.** C23's conclusion (list вЙ§ kвИТ1, ќі* past Johnson) would indeed be past
Johnson IF the fixed-ќЉ_n certificate were nonzero. It is not вАФ GM-MDS provides no closed argument that
reaches past Johnson here. **Why not a hidden open Lovett lemma.** `lovettThm17_unconditional` and
`lovettPrimitiveCase_unconditional` are proven sorry-free in tree, so the GM-MDS engine itself is
closed; the conjecture's "LovettPrimitiveCase reduced-not-closed" is outdated. The genuinely missing
step is fixed-ќЉ_n nonvanishing in the past-Johnson regime вАФ the open structured/p-dependent bound вАФ
and for the GM-MDS certificate it is provably FALSE. So GM-MDS cannot be the off-BGK lever; matches
the route-elimination meta-theorem (closed/cyclotomic objects cap at Johnson).

Probe: `scripts/probes/probe_c23_gmmds_fixed_vs_generic.py` (commit `ce07c4033`). Python-only; no Lean changed.

## 2026-06-14 (#444 C42): SECRETLY-OPEN вАФ CZ25 Subspace-Design List-Recovery Coordinate-Fiber Cap (R2)

**Conjecture C42 (gmmds-homds, feas 4).** Via the CZ25 subspace-design framework, claim the
ќі-close codewords on a far line span dimension вЙ§ r (`CZ25CoordFiberCap`), so list-recovery gives
a constant list PAST Johnson; the determining-tuple existence is axiom-clean in-tree. REDUCES-TO:
CZ25 subspace-design list-recovery + in-tree `exists_determining_tuple` (axiom-clean) +
`CZ25CoordFiberCap`.

**Verdict: secretly-open** (and where actually proven in-tree, only reaches Johnson). The
load-bearing claim "the ќі-close codewords span dim вЙ§ r" IS the in-tree OPEN Prop
`CZ25CoordFiberCap`, not a theorem. This is a clean structural/logical horn вАФ no ќЉ_n number-theory
counterexample needed; the audit is of the in-tree CZ25 / GuruswamiвАУWang chain itself.

**The audit (all from axiom-clean in-tree files).**
1. `CZ25CoordFiberCap` is a `def вА¶ : Prop` (a named hypothesis), the affine-flat coordinate-fiber
   cap `вИС_i #{cвИИL : c_i=f_i} вЙ§ ((|L|вИТ1)¬ЈѕД(rвВА)+1)¬Јn`
   (`ListDecoding/CZ25SpanBoundBridge.lean:92`). The workbench
   (`PROXIMITY_PRIZE_WORKBENCH.lean:142`) lists it verbatim as the **R2 GAP**.
2. The ONLY unconditional, axiom-clean discharge of `CZ25CoordFiberCap` in-tree is
   `cz25CoordFiberCap_of_ncard_le_one` (`ListDecoding/CZ25UniqueDecodingSlice.lean:80`), whose
   hypothesis `hle` is "every candidate list has вЙ§ 1 codeword" = the **unique-decoding /
   sub-Johnson** regime. Past Johnson a coordinate fiber is a full affine flat of size `q^{dim} вЙЂ
   dim+1` (the documented "q^dim vs dim+1" obstruction, `CZ25SpanDimension.lean:292вАУ302`); the cap
   is then OPEN. (Probe: at q=2^30, dimвЙ•2 the flat exceeds the naive linear cap by >1e17√Ч.)
3. Every other in-tree path is conditional `*_of_*`:
   `cz25CoordFiberCap_of_interp_and_multiplicity` вЯЄ {BRICK-I, BRICK-V, BRICK-W, BRICK-L}, and:
   - **BRICK-W** (`GWDirectionFinrankLe`) was MACHINE-REFUTED as false for genuine codes
     (see "R2 / GW kernel mis-wiring catch" entry above; `GWDirectionScopedWiring.lean`).
   - **BRICK-L** (`GWAffineFiberCharge`) was REFUTED as literally stated (empty close list вЗТ
     `finrank вЙ§ вИТ1`) and re-reduced to `GWFiberCardCharge`, whose own honesty note states it is
     "**as deep as the GW capacity theorem itself вА¶ the irreducible algebraic kernel**"
     (`GWFiberChargeRepair.lean:52вАУ59`); discharged ONLY on the singleton/sub-Johnson slice
     (`GWFiberCardCharge_holds_of_singleton`).
4. `exists_determining_tuple` IS axiom-clean (as C42 says) вАФ but it **consumes** the dimension
   bound as a hypothesis `hr : finrank F H вЙ§ r`; it does NOT produce it
   (`SubspaceDesignLineDecodable.lean:81`). So the "span dim вЙ§ r" C42 calls closed is the *input*,
   supplied by the OPEN `CZ25CoordFiberCap`.
5. Framework mismatch: this is the FOLDED-RS / s-folded subspace-design route (carrier
   `ќє вЖТ Fin s вЖТ F`, hypothesis `IsSubspaceDesign s ѕД C`). The PRIZE object is PLAIN RS over an
   explicit ќЉ_n (s=1), where the design profile collapses (ABF26 Lemma 2.17 repair: `ѕД вЙ• ѕБвИТ1/n`)
   and the worst-case far-line incidence is not controlled by any nonvacuous design budget вАФ
   exactly GG25's plain-RS gap (R1).

**Mechanism.** C42 reduces past-Johnson list-decoding to `CZ25CoordFiberCap`, the named OPEN
in-tree Prop (= the irreducible GW capacity kernel, "as deep as the whole theorem"), proven ONLY
on the sub-Johnson / unique-decoding slice (`|L| вЙ§ 1`). The "span dim вЙ§ r" is the open input, not
a closed fact. Hidden open step = the affine-flat coordinate-fiber cap past Johnson.

Probe: `scripts/probes/probe_c42_cz25_coordfibercap_open.py` (structural audit + q^dim-vs-dim+1).

## C01 "Antipodal Sumset Saturation Pin" (Lam-Leung + Pl√Љnnecke-Ruzsa): REFUTED вАФ offset is n/4, NOT logвВВ(n) вАФ 2026-06-14

**Conjecture C01:** the bad-scalar far-line crossing obeys `w вЙ§ k + logвВВ(n/gcd(bвИТa,n))`; maximizing
over `gcd(bвИТa,n)=1` (`d=n/gcd=n`) pins `ќі* = (1вИТѕБ) вИТ logвВВ(n)/n`, claimed to beat Johnson for all
`ѕБвИИ{1/2,1/4,1/8,1/16}` once `nвЙ•16`. Claimed reduction: in-tree Lam-Leung coset/isolated split +
Beukers-Smyth isolated count + Pl√Љnnecke-Ruzsa `|rA|вЙ§K^r|A|`.

**Verdict: refuted-false.** C01 IS the already-killed `ќі*=(1вИТѕБ)вИТlogвВВ(n)/n` candidate (commit
`ce8cb602e`, n=64) re-stated with an antipodal-sumset *mechanism*; the mechanism's own structural
prediction (`offset = logвВВ(n/gcd)`) is contradicted by the exact char-0 far-line incidence.

**Decisive datum вАФ the crossing offset `s*вИТk` is `n/4` (LINEAR), not `logвВВ(n)`:** exact char-0
`(k+1)`-subset divided-difference engine (= in-tree `probe_char0_deltastar_n64_BIG.py`), proper
subgroup `ќЉ_n<F_p*`, `p` prime, `pвЙЂn¬≥`, NEVER `n=pвИТ1`, two primes per point:

| n  | k | ѕБ    | measured `s*вИТk` | C01 `logвВВ(n)` | campaign `n/4` | ќі* (=3/4вИТѕБ) |
|----|---|------|-----------------|---------------|----------------|-------------|
| 16 | 2 | 1/8  | **4**           | 4             | 4              | 0.62500     |
| 32 | 2 | 1/16 | **8**           | 5             | 8              | 0.68750     |
| 64 | 2 | 1/32 | **16**          | 6             | 16             | 0.71875     |

At `n=16` `logвВВ16 = 16/4 = 4` *coincide* вАФ that accidental tie is the entire basis of C01-B. They
diverge at `n=32` (5 vs 8; data=8) and blow apart at `n=64` (6 vs 16; data=16, off by 10). The
worst `n=64` pencil is `(2,34)` (`bвИТa=32=n/2`, `gcd=32`, `d=2`), NOT a `gcd=1` direction вАФ so even
C01-A's "maximizer is `gcd=1`, `d=n`" premise fails at `n=64`; and where a `gcd=1` pencil ties for
the offset (`n=32`, pencil `(8,17)`), its offset is `8=n/4`, NOT `logвВВ(d)=logвВВ32=5`. Either way the
`logвВВ(n/gcd)` bound is loose by `ќШ(n)`.

**Which horn:** `ќі*=3/4вИТѕБ` is a CONSTANT 1/4 below capacity (`s*вИТk=n/4` is linear, so the gap
`(capвИТќі*)=1/4` is `ѕБ`-uniform and does NOT shrink). This is the rigorous char-0 far-line UPPER bound
OFF the BGK wall вАФ it does not track the `ќШ(1/log n)` floor at all, and `ќі*=(1вИТѕБ)вИТlogвВВ(n)/n` is
simply a wrong (too-aggressive-toward-capacity) value. The Pl√Љnnecke-Ruzsa `K^r` multiplier the
conjecture invokes is exactly the slack that makes `logвВВ(n/gcd)` an over-tight (false) bound rather
than the true `n/4`: restricted subset-sums of antipodal cosets do NOT saturate at coset size in the
window interior; the bad set is a free-interpolation arc (consistent with the P4 Mann/Lam-Leung
refutation at `DISPROOF_LOG` "char-0 I_0(ќі) has NO clean ѕБ-uniform CLOSED FORM").

**p-independence (char-0 faithful):** worstI band identical across the two primes at n=16,32; the
small `¬±32` deltas at n=32/64 in raw counts are the `+1`/`ќ≥=0`-edge multiplicity wobble, irrelevant
to the integer offset (always exactly 4,8,16).

**Probes committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_c01_worst_direction.py` (flushed; gcd-class offset
table n=16,32, dual-prime), `scripts/probes/probe_c01_antipodal_sumset_pin.py` (general engine);
re-confirmed with the in-tree `probe_char0_deltastar_n64_BIG.py --n 64 --k 2`
(`w_cross=18, s*вИТk=16=n/4, ќі*=0.71875`). Consistent with commit `ce8cb602e`.

## [C21] (gmmds-homds) Affine-General-Position Restriction: ќі*=capacity via genpos far-line incidence вАФ REFUTED

**Claim:** the worst-case far-line candidate list (bad-alpha messages of `RS[k]` that
ќі-agree with `x^a+ќ± x^b`) is **affinely independent** in the window interior, so the
in-tree `mds_genpos_list_bound` caps it at `L<budget`, pinning `ќі*вЙ•1вИТѕБвИТc/log n` past
Johnson. Defense: "affine independence is forced because any low-degree ќЉ_n-supported
vanishing is capped at `2(kвИТ1)<a` roots by Mann/Conway-Jones."

**Verdict: refuted-false.** The premise confuses two unrelated objects. `mds_genpos_list_bound`
(`ArkLib/Data/CodingTheory/HigherOrderMDSListGenPos.lean:104`) requires `hindep :
LinearIndependent K (fun j => m_{j+1} вИТ m_0)` вАФ affine independence of the **candidate
message list** in the `k`-dimensional message space. That has nothing to do with root
counts of ќЉ_n-supported vanishings; it is hard-capped by `dim = k`: any set of messages
(deg `< k` polys) has affine rank `вЙ§ k+1`. The moment the list exceeds `k+1` elements вАФ
which it does massively in the deep window interior вАФ the family is **necessarily affinely
DEPENDENT** and `mds_genpos_list_bound` is vacuous. This is exactly the affinely-dependent
line/incidence case the in-tree docstrings flag as the open higher-order-MDS direction
(`HigherOrderMDSListGenPos.lean:24-26`, `LineListBound.lean:28-29`,
`MonomialLineListBridge.lean:37-38`: "the RS[k+1] list concentrates to its first moment,
i.e. the list-decoding prize itself").

**Decisive datum вАФ list size I vs affine rank R, exact over F_p, p>>n^3, ќЉ_n PROPER subgroup,
NEVER n=pвИТ1** (`scripts/probes/probe_c21_genpos_affine_rank.py`, commit `545a978274`):

| n  | k | pencil (a,b) | thr (deep interior) | list I | affine rank R | cap k+1 |
|----|---|--------------|---------------------|--------|---------------|---------|
| 8  | 2 | (3,2)        | 3                   | **40** | 3             | 3       |
| 8  | 2 | (4,2)        | 3                   | 17     | 3             | 3       |
| 8  | 4 | (5,4)        | 5                   | **40** | 5             | 5       |
| 8  | 4 | (6,4)        | 5                   | 20     | 5             | 5       |
| 16 | 4 | (5,4)        | 5                   | **2256**| 5            | 5       |

Everywhere the deep-interior list is nontrivial, `I >> R` and `R` saturates at exactly
`k+1` (the affine-rank cap). The worst-case list is affinely dependent by a factor of
`I/R = 13.3, 5.7, 8, 4, 451`. C21's affine-independence premise is false precisely where
it is needed.

**Which horn:** secretly-open masquerading as closed via a false premise. C21's only
beyond-Johnson lever (`mds_genpos_list_bound`) is **vacuous on the worst-case object**;
the real worst case is the affinely-dependent far-line incidence = the open BGK / line-ball
core (`OpenCoreConditionalPin.WorstCaseIncidenceBounded`). Mann/Conway-Jones caps the
INNER per-witness ќЉ_n-root count, not the OUTER affine rank of the bad-alpha message family;
conflating them is the error. Consistent with the route-elimination meta-theorem (genpos
caps at Johnson; past Johnson needs the open p-dependent BGK moment) and with
`probe_407_lacunary_pencil_Npencil.py` (deep-interior I jumps far above budget n).

**Probe committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_c21_genpos_affine_rank.py` (commit `545a978274`).

## C20 (#444): Khovanskii fewnomial real-analogue + char-faithful complexification вАФ REFUTED (false premise) вАФ 2026-06-15

**Claim.** The ragged (non-coset) ќЉ_n-roots of a `(k+2)`-nomial `f=X^a+ќ≥X^bвИТh` (`deg h<k`)
are bounded by Khovanskii's `n,p`-INDEPENDENT fewnomial count `2^{C(k+2,2)}(k+2)`, so
`s* вЙ§ core(n/2)+const(n)`, giving `ќі*=1/2` past Johnson for `ѕБ<1/4`. Coset stripping and char
transfer cited as in-tree proven (`FactorizationRigidity`, `RigidityReductionPrizeScale`).

**The fatal premise is false.** Khovanskii's fewnomial theorem bounds **POSITIVE REAL**
solutions (a degree-free count, valid because real sign-variation is limited). There is **no
real-analogue over fields with roots of unity**: the number of roots of a `t`-nomial in
`ќЉ_n вКВ F_p` (or even `ќЉ_n вКВ вДВ`) is governed by the **DEGREE**, not the monomial count.

**Machine/numeric witness (extends in-tree `_RThinSqrtNKRefuted.lean` to prize-band primes).**
The `k=2` family `f=(X^m+1)(XвИТ1)=X^{m+1}вИТX^m+XвИТ1` is a `(k+2)=4`-nomial (support `{0,1,m,m+1}`).
Its `ќЉ_{2m}`-root set `S={x:x^m=вИТ1}вИ™{1}` is **genuinely ragged** (coeff at `X^1` is `вЙ†0` вЯє not a
`ќЉ_{d'}`-coset-union for any `d'>1`, by factorization rigidity) of size `m+1 = n/2+1`. Over prize
primes (`p` prime, `pвЙ°1 mod n`, `p>n^3`, `n<p^{1/4}`, dyadic `n=2^ќЉ`, NEVER `n=pвИТ1`):

| n | m | p | ragged ќЉ_n-root count | Khovanskii cap `2^C(4,2)¬Ј4` | violation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 512 | 256 | 68719484929 | **257** | 256 | yes |
| 1024 | 512 | 1099511630849 | **513** | 256 | yes |
| 2048 | 1024 | 17592186062849 | **1025** | 256 | yes |
| 4096 | 2048 | 281474976768001 | **2049** | 256 | yes |

The count is exactly `m+1`, ќШ(n); the alleged "constant" cap `256` is a fixed number вАФ the gap
is unbounded. Violation begins at `n=512`.

**Why even the "salvageable" reading fails to reach past Johnson.** Per C20's own coset-stripping,
suppose one only bounds the ragged EXCESS over the largest coset core. The probe shows
`S = (ќЉ_m-coset of size n/2) вИ™ {one straggler}`: the excess is `1=O(k)` (correct, and exactly the
in-tree `ragged_excess_le_degree`), but the **coset core itself is n/2** = the antipodal `ќЉ_{n/2}`
coset, i.e. the **Johnson-radius-sized** object. So `s* вЙИ core = n/2` вЯє `ќі* = 1вИТѕБ`?? No: the
core IS the closed/antipodal/cyclotomic object the route-elimination meta-theorem proves caps at
**Johnson** (`1вИТвИЪѕБ`). Khovanskii contributes nothing to the core, and the core is where all the
mass is. C20 gives no past-Johnson lever.

**Which horn:** refuted-false (false load-bearing premise вАФ Khovanskii has no roots-of-unity
analogue), degenerating to reduces-to-johnson on the salvageable reading. Consistent with the
prior DISPROOF_LOG entry **wf-NK (#444)** (fewnomial adds nothing tighter than the `C(n,size)`
union bound; Tao-uncertainty needs `n` prime, vacuous for `n=2^ќЉ`).

**Probes committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_c20_khovanskii_ragged_root_count.py`,
`scripts/probes/probe_c20_khovanskii_ragged_certificate.py` (commit `fb3785b58`).

## C17 (#444) вАФ Kelley-Owen Dilation-Pencil "Sharp Constant" s* вЙ§ вИЪ((k+1)n) as EQUALITY: REFUTED вЖТ reduces-to-johnson

**Claim.** Generalize the proven trinomial bound (`¬љ+вИЪn` roots, `t=3`, `k=1`) to `(k+2)`-term
faces via the `k`-dimensional dilation pencil, asserting the sharp double-count gives
`s* вЙ§ вИЪ((k+1)n) + O(k)` as an **EQUALITY realized by the antipodal pencil**, pinning `ќі*` exactly
at `1вИТвИЪѕБ(1+o(1))`. Reduces to in-tree `KelleyOwenDilationPencil.pencil_card_core` +
`stepanov_sqrt_bound` + Heath-Brown-Konyagin Stepanov for the `k`-term case.

**Why it falls (two horns at once).**

1. **The "sharp equality / upper bound" is FALSE for the `(k+2)`-face.** The in-tree
   machine-checked witness `_RThinSqrtNKRefuted` is exactly a `(k+2)`-sparse face (`k=2`):
   `P(X) = (X^m+1)(XвИТ1) = X^{m+1} вИТ X^m + X вИТ 1`, support `{0,1,m,m+1}` (`t=4=k+2`),
   `m=n/2`. Its root set in `ќЉ_n` is `{x : x^m=вИТ1} вИ™ {1}`, of size `n/2+1` вАФ **linear in `n`,
   not `вИЪn`**, and `> вИЪ((k+1)n)` for every `nвЙ•8`. So the dilation-pencil double-count is not
   even a valid worst-case upper bound (let alone a tight equality): the `k`-dimensional pencil's
   blocks overlap in a whole coset core, `M(S) вЙ• n/2вИТ1` by `PencilAutocorrelation.autocorr_ge_coset_core`,
   and the Corr√°di/Fisher count collapses to `r вЙ§ ќШ(n)`. The antipodal pencil does NOT realize
   `вИЪ((k+1)n)`; it realizes `n/2`. Probe `probe_c17_dilation_pencil_sharp.py` reproduces
   `|S|=n/2+1` over proper dyadic `ќЉ_n`, `p` prime, `p~n^4 вЙЂ n^3`, `nвЙ†pвИТ1` (commit `b73fa70e8`).

2. **Even where the bound IS an upper bound, it lands EXACTLY at Johnson.** With `k=ѕБn`,
   `вИЪ((k+1)n) ~ вИЪ(ѕБn¬Јn) = nвИЪѕБ` = the Johnson agreement count, i.e. radius `1вИТвИЪѕБ` = the Johnson
   radius. The conjecture's own stated conclusion ("pins `ќі*` at `1вИТвИЪѕБ(1+o(1))`") is the Johnson
   radius verbatim. The `вИЪ` upgrade special to `t=3` (1-D pencil, overlap-1) degrades to `вИЪ(kn)`
   for `t=k+2` (`k`-D pencil, overlap-`k`) вАФ documented in `_KelleyOwenDilationPencil.lean`
   ¬І"Honest scope". Heath-Brown-Konyagin Stepanov gives the same `вИЪ(kn)`; it never reaches the
   `n`-independent isolated-excess budget (`Kelley Conj 3.2 / KO Heuristic 1.4`, recognized open).

**Which horn:** `reduces-to-johnson` (the in-tree bricks it cites are real and axiom-clean, but
they prove only the `t=3` `вИЪn` rung and the `t=k+2` `вИЪ(kn)`=Johnson degradation), with the
sharp-equality premise outright `refuted-false` by `_RThinSqrtNKRefuted`. Same family and same
witness as the prior **C20 (Khovanskii ragged root count)** entry above. Khovanskii/HBK Stepanov
adds nothing past Johnson; the coset core (the closed/antipodal object) is where the mass is and
caps at Johnson by the route-elimination meta-theorem.

**Probe committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_c17_dilation_pencil_sharp.py` (commit `b73fa70e8`).

## C19 (#444): Mason-Stothers ABC degree-multiplicity pin via antipodal square descent вАФ REFUTED (vacuous proof mechanism; the bound it claims to deliver IS the open core) вАФ 2026-06-15

**Claim.** After antipodal even/odd descent, the isolated (ragged) witnesses inject into the
`ќЉ_{n/2}`-roots of `F = E¬≤вИТu¬ЈO¬≤` with `E = u^{a/2}+ќ≥u^{b/2}вИТcE`, `deg E,O < k/2`. C19 asserts
**Mason-Stothers** (`Polynomial.abc` in Mathlib) on the coprime triple `X=E¬≤, Y=вИТu¬ЈO¬≤, Z=F`
(`X+Y=Z`) with *small* `rad(u¬ЈO¬≤)` forces the distinct-root count `вЙ§ 3k/2+1`, flat in `n`, giving
`ќі*=1/2вИТO(ѕБ)` past Johnson at `ѕБ=1/16`. Cites in-tree `_IsoSparsityMasonStothers` (`O(k)` sparsity)
+ `_AntipodalEvenOddDescent`.

**The proof mechanism is VACUOUS вАФ Mason-Stothers never beats the trivial `deg F` bound on the
ragged triple.** MS gives `max(deg X,deg Y,deg Z) вЙ§ rad(X¬ЈY¬ЈZ)вИТ1 вЙ§ rad(E)+rad(u¬ЈO¬≤)+rad(F)вИТ1`.
For the prize shape with `a,b ~ n`:
- `deg F = deg(E¬≤) = a вЙИ n` (trivial bound);
- `rad(E) вЙИ (aвИТb)/2 вЙИ n/2` вАФ the sparse binomial head `u^{a/2}+ќ≥u^{b/2}` re-injects an `вЙИ n/2`
  radical that `deg O<k/2` does **not** control;
- `rad(u¬ЈO¬≤) вЙ§ 1+deg O < 1+k/2` вАФ the ONLY small radical (the only `deg O<k/2` lever);
- `rad(F) вЙИ deg F вЙИ n` вАФ in the genuinely-**ragged** case `F` is squarefree of full degree.

So MS reads `a вЙ§ (a/2)+(k/2)+aвИТ1 = (3a/2)+(k/2)вИТ1 вЙЂ a`. The two `вЙИ n` radicals `rad(E)~n/2`,
`rad(F)~n` DROWN the small `rad(u¬ЈO¬≤)`. This is exactly the in-tree analytic statement
`mason_stothers_iso_feed_vacuous` (`_IsoSparsityMasonStothers.lean:122`), whose own docstring says
"MS-direct yields a *proven* O(k) sparsity, and **reduces** |iso|вЙ§poly(k) to a named isolated /
non-coset root conjecture; it does **not** beat the general Kelley/BGK count."

**Machine/numeric witness (exact over GF(p), proper `ќЉ_n`, `p` prime, `p>n¬≥`, NEVER `n=pвИТ1`).**
Built extremal prize-shape `P=x^a+ќ≥x^bвИТc` (a,b even, deg c<k) planting `k+1` agreement points,
ran the antipodal descent, computed actual polynomial radicals (squarefree parts) of `E¬≤`, `u¬ЈO¬≤`,
`F` over `F_p`, and tested `rad(E)+rad(u¬ЈO¬≤)+rad(F)вИТ1 < deg F` (= "MS beats trivial"):

| n | k | a | b | OвЙ°0? | degF | radE¬≤ | rad(uO¬≤) | radF | MS rhs | MS beats? |
|---|---|---|---|------|------|-------|----------|------|--------|-----------|
| 64 | 4 | 28 | 24 | no (ragged) | 28 | 14 | 2 | 28 | 43 | **no** |
| 64 | 8 | 56 | 40 | no (ragged) | 56 | 28 | 4 | 54 | 85 | **no** |
| 128 | 8 | 86 | 14 | no (ragged) | 86 | 43 | 4 | 85 | 131 | **no** |
| 128 | 16 | 86 | 34 | no (ragged) | 86 | 43 | 8 | 86 | 136 | **no** |

In **every genuinely-ragged (`OвЙҐ0`) row вАФ the ONLY case C19's mechanism targets** вАФ MS rhs вЙИ 3n/2
вЙЂ deg F: MS is strictly vacuous. The only rows where MS "beats" the trivial bound are the
**pure-coset `OвЙ°0` case** (then `F=E¬≤` is a perfect square, `radF=radE¬≤=n/2`, `rad(uO¬≤)=0`), which
is **NOT** the ragged/isolated witnesses C19 needs вАФ it is the in-tree closed-form antipodal case
the route-elimination meta-theorem already proves **caps at Johnson**. And even there MS only
recovers `root count вЙИ n/2`, nowhere near the claimed `3k/2+1`.

**The deeper structural point.** C19 conflates (i) the *measured* fact `|iso| вЙ§ 3k/2+1` flat in n
(real вАФ confirmed by probes here and by `_AntipodalEvenOddDescent`'s `IsolatedCountResidual`) with
(ii) a *proof* that Mason-Stothers delivers it (false). The bound `|iso| вЙ§ poly(k)` is **exactly**
the open isolated/non-coset root count = the recognised Kelley/BGK general-position cancellation one
2-adic level down (`IsolatedCountResidual`, `IsolatedNonCosetCountResidual`), which both in-tree
bricks explicitly NAME as the open residual they do NOT discharge. Mason-Stothers touches none of
it: the `T`-sparsity of `F` does not bound roots in a subgroup (`u^eвИТ1` is 2-sparse with `e` roots),
and the MS radical bound is drowned by the sparse-square head.

**Which horn:** **secretly-open** вАФ the bound C19 claims Mason-Stothers proves IS the open BGK/Kelley
isolated-root count; MS is provably vacuous on the ragged triple, so the past-Johnson pin requires
the unresolved residual. Degenerates to **reduces-to-johnson** on the only reading where MS is
non-vacuous (the pure-coset `OвЙ°0` antipodal core, capped at Johnson by the meta-theorem). Consistent
with **C20 (#444)** (no roots-of-unity Khovanskii analogue), **wf-NK (#444)** (fewnomial adds
nothing tighter than the union bound), and the in-tree `mason_stothers_iso_feed_vacuous` /
`IsolatedNonCosetCountResidual` self-documented gap.

**Probe committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_conjecture_refute_C19_mason_stothers.py` (self-contained,
sympy GF(p); exact radicals + ragged/pure-coset split).

## C32 (#444) вАФ BGK Sum-Product Sup-Norm via BSG + 2-power antipodal collision вАФ **SECRETLY-OPEN**

**Claim.** The BGK sum-product bound `M(ќЉ_n) вЙ§ n^{1вИТo(1)}` (ineffective) can be *sharpened* to the
prize target `вИЪ(n log(p/n))` past Johnson by combining the BalogвАУSzemer√©diвАУGowers (BSG) structure of
low-energy subgroups with the proven antipodal-only (`z + (вИТz) = 0`) collision structure at 2-power.

**Horn: secretly-open.** This conjecture *is the open core itself* (the prize statement
`max_{bвЙ†0}|ќ£_{xвИИќЉ_n} e_p(bx)| вЙ§ вИЪ(2n log m)`, see `the-prize-isolated-direct-bgk-statement.md`),
re-stated with a *named proof strategy* attached. The strategy (BSG + antipodal) supplies **nothing
new**; the half-power gap remains exactly the named open BGK sup-norm bound.

**Why BSG is vacuous here (machine witness, proper `ќЉ_n`, `p` prime, `p вЙЂ n¬≥`, NEVER `n=pвИТ1`).**
BSG is a *high-energy* theorem: it extracts an approximate subgroup from a set whose additive energy is
*large*. But `ќЉ_n` is at the **minimum-energy endpoint** вАФ it is Sidon, and *jointly* Sidon with every
dilate. Measured exactly over GF(p):

| n | p | p/n¬≥ | M | log M / log n | prize вИЪ(2n log(p/n)) | E¬≤(ќЉ_n,ќЊќЉ_n) | diagonal n¬≤ |
|---|---|------|---|----------------|----------------------|--------------|-------------|
| 8  | 5009   | 9.78 | 7.48  | **0.968** | 10.15 | 64   | 64   |
| 16 | 16417  | 4.01 | 13.11 | **0.928** | 14.90 | 256  | 256  |
| 32 | 131297 | 4.01 | 19.21 | **0.853** | 23.08 | 1024 | 1024 |

- **(A)** The true sup-norm sits at `log M / log n вЙИ 0.85вАУ0.97 = BGK SOTA (n^{1вИТo(1)})`, **not 1/2**.
  No square-root cancellation is visible; the prize exponent `1/2` is nowhere in the measured data.
- **(BвА≤)** The joint dilate energy `EвБЇ(ќЉ_n, ќЊќЉ_n)` is **diagonal-only = n¬≤** for every tested dilate ќЊ
  вИЙ ќЉ_n. So `ќЉ_n` is already the *best possible* additive input. BSG's hypothesis ("large energy вЗТ
  approximate subgroup") is vacuously satisfied: **there is no excess energy to harvest.** BSG +
  best-possible energy gives **zero exponent gain** вАФ consistent with Kowalski 2024 (arXiv:2401.04756)
  and dual-assault L8: the BGK losses are **regime-driven (n = p^ќ≥, ќ≥ < 1/2), not seed-energy-driven.**
- **(C)** The sup-norm-from-energy conversion `M^{2r} вЙ§ ќ£_b|ќЈ_b|^{2r} = p¬ЈE_r` needs near-Gaussian `E_r`
  up to `r ~ log p`. Measured `E_r/((2rвИТ1)!!n ≥)` departs from the Gaussian baseline already at small r
  (char-p wrap excess), and BSG/sum-product controls **only low-order (r=2,3)** energy. The deep-moment
  object at `r ~ log p` is exactly the named open residual, untouched by BSG.

**The structural point.** The antipodal-only collision structure (Mann/ConwayвАУJones, in-tree
`AntipodalBalanceBounded`) governs which char-0 vanishing relations exist; it provably **caps at
Johnson** (route-elimination meta-theorem). Combining a Johnson-capped closed object (antipodal) with
a high-energy theorem (BSG) whose hypothesis is vacuous on a Sidon set produces no past-Johnson lever.
The "sharpening" is just the original open BGK bound under a new name. The half-power `вИЪ(p)/n` deficit
is the recognised ~25-year-open analytic-NT problem (di Benedetto `n^{0.989}` needs `p^{1/4}<H<p^{1/2}`,
*outside* the prize band `n вЙ™ вИЪp`).

**Probe committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_c32_bsg_antipodal_supnorm.py` (self-contained, sympy GF(p);
exact sup-norm, additive/dilate energy, r-th energy vs Gaussian). Commit `4bdcc12ea`.
